title: onobrychis pulchella
intro: Onobrychis pulchella, also known as the lesser birdsfoot or smaller birdsfoot trefoil, is a plant species in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and as an antiseptic. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Onobrychis pulchella is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to support the immune system and alleviate various health issues.
